primary_finding: SOLUSDT destroys the portfolio (edge −0.962 bps, WR 3%) via two compounding defects: strategy.py fails to enforce the spec OBI threshold (avg_entry_obi=−0.028 vs gate 0.749589) AND SOL spread 1.17 bps structurally exceeds profit_target 1.09 bps, meaning even a correctly gated SOL entry would be unwinnable; BTC and ETH independently demonstrate genuine edge (+0.267 / +0.184 bps) with time_stop-primary architecture intact, but 94.7% fee_to_edge ratio at 4 bps taker makes the strategy non-deployable without a maker execution path.
agreement_points: SOLUSDT is the sole source of portfolio drag (−0.962 bps per trade, −481 bps aggregate) and must be excluded or fixed before any portfolio-level assessment is meaningful
BTC and ETH independently show positive edge (+0.267 and +0.184 bps respectively) with WR 37–38%, consistent with the signal brief's avg_ic=0.25 for OBI_1 × fwd_10t
time_stop as primary exit (91.3% of exits) is architecturally correct — BTC/ETH time_stop averages are positive (+0.147 / +0.113 bps), matching the brief's design intent
At real Binance taker fee of 4 bps round-trip, fee_to_edge_ratio = 94.7% — the strategy is non-deployable with MARKET entry orders at any current fee tier
disagreement_points: Alpha-critic frames SOL's failure primarily as a strategy.py implementation bug (OBI threshold bypass: all 500 SOL entries have OBI below gate); execution-critic frames it as a structural entry cost problem (spread 1.17 bps > PT 1.09 bps, which would make SOL losses inevitable even with a correct OBI gate) — both framings are valid and the fixes are complementary, not alternatives

Alpha-critic notes OBI separation (WIN 0.869 vs LOSS 0.469, delta +0.401) is a promising signal; execution-critic correctly observes this delta is entirely a SOL-vs-BTC/ETH artifact rather than within-symbol OBI discriminating power — both are right; true within-symbol OBI signal quality for SOL remains unassessed until the threshold bypass is fixed
local_seed: Fix strategy.py SOL OBI threshold bypass (enforce `obi >= obi_thresholds[symbol]` guard at entry for all symbols); add per-symbol spread gate rejecting entry when `spread_bps >= profit_target_bps` (this eliminates all 500 SOL structural losses and any symbol with spread > 1.09 bps); enable track_mfe=true for intra-path MFE/MAE data; rerun lob_iter2 with BTC/ETH/SOL — expect BTC+ETH portfolio edge ~+0.226 bps at fee=0 and SOL naturally excluded via spread gate
escape_seed: Pivot to passive LIMIT maker entry design: post bid-side resting limit at mid or mid−0.5 bps, wait for fill within 3 ticks before cancelling; this reduces entry fee from taker 4 bps to near-zero maker (potentially negative rebate), making BTC+ETH edge (+0.226 bps gross) viable net of fees; requires queue-position model using LOB depth levels 2–5 to estimate fill probability before posting — holding_duration remains time_stop at 10 ticks, but exit switches to LIMIT at mid+PT to also capture maker exit rebate
next_idea_seed: Fix strategy.py SOL OBI threshold bypass (enforce `obi >= obi_thresholds[symbol]` guard at entry for all symbols); add per-symbol spread gate rejecting entry when `spread_bps >= profit_target_bps` (this eliminates all 500 SOL structural losses and any symbol with spread > 1.09 bps); enable track_mfe=true for intra-path MFE/MAE data; rerun lob_iter2 with BTC/ETH/SOL — expect BTC+ETH portfolio edge ~+0.226 bps at fee=0 and SOL naturally excluded via spread gate
structural_concern: At real Binance taker fee (4 bps round-trip), fee_to_edge_ratio = 94.7% against BTC+ETH gross edge of 0.226 bps — MARKET entry is fundamentally incompatible with a 1-second LOB signal horizon at any realistic fee tier; iter 2 should fix the implementation bugs to confirm the genuine BTC/ETH edge, but iter 3 must address fee structure by switching to passive LIMIT maker entry or the strategy can never be deployed
data_requests: tick-by-tick mid-price trajectory for 10 ticks after each entry fill (enable track_mfe=true in engine) — required for MFE/MAE capture_pct analysis; 97.6% of trades have null MFE/MAE in this run

per-symbol spread_bps distribution across the full IS window (not just at entry) — to calibrate spread gate threshold per-symbol rather than relying on brief's single-value estimate; SOL brief estimate was 0.065 bps, actual = 1.17 bps (18× underestimate)

n_resting_cancelled=1380 explanation — for a MARKET-entry strategy, clarify whether these are exit-leg LIMIT order cancellations at time_stop or internal order tracking artifacts; if exit is LIMIT not MARKET, realized exit prices may differ from bid-based PnL calculation

OBI threshold adherence logs per entry — split time_stop pnl_bps by threshold-compliant vs sub-threshold entries for BTC/ETH to isolate true OBI signal quality from the 38.6%/58% sub-threshold entries that currently contaminate the WR estimates

extensions.clean_pnl_gate: not_run — attribute_pnl.py not executed; invariant_violations=0 so total_pnl=0.1017% is all genuine (no bug_pnl from invariant violations); however, the OBI threshold bypass is a strategy.py logic bug not caught by the spec-invariant checker (checker only monitors position/SL/PT/time bounds, not signal gate compliance)
extensions.invariant_classification: 0 formal violations; the OBI threshold bypass is a signal implementation defect outside the spec-invariant checker scope — it is not recorded in invariant_violation_by_type but is equally damaging (500 unintended trades)
extensions.sol_spread_vs_pt: 1.17 bps spread > 1.09 bps PT — structurally blocks all SOL entries
extensions.sol_obi_at_entry_vs_gate: avg_entry_obi=-0.028 vs spec gate 0.749589 — threshold bypass confirmed
extensions.iter2_expected_outcome: With SOL OBI gate fixed + spread gate added: portfolio reduces to BTC+ETH only (500+500 trades), expected avg_edge ~+0.226 bps at fee=0; SOL excluded by spread gate (1.17 > 1.09)
